Title: Define cross-skill handoff contract
Description: Define the minimal capture envelope and field semantics that let the new browser-capture skill compose cleanly with `html-to-markdown` without repo-local coupling
Acceptance: A single documented contract exists for `html`, `url`, `final_url`, and `content_type`, with required vs optional fields and output-mode expectations made explicit
Test_Method: manual: review the new plan and the skill references to confirm one stable handoff contract

Title: Choose general skill name and scope
Description: Name the new skill by its job rather than the backend library, and define a narrow v1 scope that is about URL-to-HTML capture rather than general browser automation
Acceptance: The new skill name is general-purpose, the v1 scope is explicitly HTML capture only, and the scope excludes parser logic and broad browser-toolkit ambitions
Test_Method: manual: inspect the new skill frontmatter and SKILL.md boundary language

Title: Define artifact layout and progressive disclosure
Description: Lay out the new skill with a short SKILL.md, targeted references, and one primary PEP 723 script so the skill stays token-efficient and self-explanatory
Acceptance: The skill has a clear folder structure with `SKILL.md`, `references/`, and `scripts/`, and SKILL.md only contains high-signal workflow guidance
Test_Method: manual: inspect skill folder structure and confirm SKILL.md stays lean

Title: Scaffold sibling capture skill files
Description: Create the new sibling skill next to `html-to-markdown` with the required folders and placeholder content that reflects the agreed scope
Acceptance: The new skill directory exists with `SKILL.md`, `references/usage.md`, `references/integration-patterns.md`, and one script entrypoint file
Test_Method: manual: list files under the new skill and review placeholder structure
Notes: No browser implementation yet in this row

Title: Design PEP 723 capture script interface
Description: Define the narrow CLI surface for the new self-contained capture script, including URL input, wait controls, headless controls, and output modes
Acceptance: The script interface is documented and implemented with a small argument set that supports one-shot HTML capture without exposing a broad browser-automation API
Test_Method: manual: review script help output and references/usage.md examples

Title: Implement one-shot nodriver capture backend
Description: Use `nodriver` behind the script to navigate to a URL, wait as requested, and extract final rendered HTML plus basic page context in a deterministic one-shot flow
Acceptance: The script can capture rendered HTML from a URL, return the final URL and content type context when available, and does not require a repo-local `.venv`
Test_Method: manual: run the PEP 723 script on one static page and one JS-heavy page
Notes: Use nodriver as backend, not as the skill identity

Title: Add stable output modes for chaining
Description: Support a default JSON envelope for chaining into `html-to-markdown` plus a raw HTML mode for direct piping, without changing the agreed field contract
Acceptance: `json` output contains the handoff contract fields and `html` output emits only the rendered HTML body suitable for piping to the conversion skill
Test_Method: manual: run the script in both output modes and inspect stdout shape

Title: Map wait and headless defaults conservatively
Description: Implement and document narrow wait/headless defaults that improve robustness without turning the script into a stateful browser framework
Acceptance: The script supports conservative `--wait-selector`, `--wait-text`, `--timeout`, and `--headless` behavior with clear defaults and no hidden long-lived daemon assumptions
Test_Method: manual: inspect help text and references/integration-patterns.md for default policy
Notes: Prefer one-shot capture semantics in v1

Title: Add skill artifact tests for the new capture skill
Description: Add unit coverage for skill structure, frontmatter, PEP 723 metadata, and basic cross-skill reference integrity
Acceptance: Tests verify the new skill files exist, the PEP 723 block is valid, and the companion-skill references do not regress into repo-entangled instructions

Title: Add script behavior tests with mocked browser seams
Description: Add narrow unit tests for argument parsing and output-envelope shaping without depending on live browser automation in default automated coverage
Acceptance: Tests cover script argument handling and contract shaping using mocks or seams rather than live browser E2E behavior
Test_Method: uv run python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'

Title: Wire light cross-references between both skills
Description: Update `html-to-markdown` and the new capture skill so they refer to each other as companions through the shared contract, without hard-calling each other or assuming local paths
Acceptance: Both skills mention the other only at the boundary points where handoff is relevant, and neither skill absorbs the other's responsibilities
Test_Method: manual: review both SKILL.md files and references for light-touch composition guidance

Title: Document robust composition patterns
Description: Document the preferred ways to chain capture and conversion, including JSON-envelope handoff and raw-HTML piping, while keeping guidance high-signal and general
Acceptance: References show at least two composition patterns that are general, token-efficient, and free of site-specific or repo-local assumptions
Test_Method: manual: review both skills' references for coherent examples

Title: Run manual smoke checks for static and JS-heavy pages
Description: Verify the new capture skill works on one static page and one JS-heavy page, and verify one end-to-end capture-to-convert path using the existing `html-to-markdown` skill
Acceptance: Manual smoke notes confirm the script captures HTML on both page classes and that at least one chained capture->convert flow works end to end
Test_Method: manual: run the capture script on two pages and feed one result into the html-to-markdown script
Notes: Validated with example.com and anthropic.com/engineering plus capture->convert chaining

Title: Validate planning artifacts
Description: Validate that the new plan and issue CSV are well-formed and aligned before implementation starts
Acceptance: The plan file exists, the CSV validates, and issue ordering follows the intended dependency chain
